spec_match.rationale: Calls Atomics.pause() inside a tight single-threaded loop and times the cost. That's a valid micro-timing test, but the spec's whole framing is 'use inside a spin-wait on shared memory' — running it without a SharedArrayBuffer probe is a weaker demonstration than the proposal motivates.

interactive.rationale: Spin-count input + two run buttons + live ms gauges + scrolling log of recent runs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises with/without pause comparison on per-iteration cost; delivered, including the honest note that the real wins land in a worker scenario.

edge_cases.rationale: Detects missing Atomics.pause and degrades; doesn't try the optional N argument (Atomics.pause(2), Atomics.pause(64)) so the reader never sees that the parameter affects timing. Also doesn't show the TypeError thrown by non-integer N.

references.rationale: Links to chromestatus.com/feature/5106098833719296 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, no static-card pattern, buttons inherit global styling.

openQuestions.title: Exercise the optional N argument
openQuestions.detail: Atomics.pause accepts an integer hint that biases how long the CPU pauses. Run three variants — pause(), pause(8), pause(64) — and time them to teach readers that the argument is a real knob (implementations clamp to a tens-to-hundreds-of-nanoseconds internal range).

openQuestions.title: Show the TypeError on non-integer N
openQuestions.detail: Per spec, Atomics.pause throws TypeError if N is neither undefined nor an integral Number. Add a small 'invalid arg' button that tries Atomics.pause(1.5) inside a try/catch and prints the thrown error — surfaces an edge case readers will otherwise be surprised by.

openQuestions.title: Add a comparative baseline without the accumulator work
openQuestions.detail: Both spinPlain and spinPause include an integer-accumulator that dominates the cost. The Atomics.pause overhead is hidden by that work — strip the accumulator from a third 'pause-only' run so the per-call cost is visible.

summary: Honest micro-timing demo with a clean before/after framing and a fair caveat that the real win is in a worker. Two important extensions: exercise the optional N argument (a headline part of the spec) and surface the TypeError on bad N. Otherwise it reads like one knob out of three.
